description: Generate a static browser tools catalog at build time by importing the compiled barrel and extracting metadata (name, description, icon). The catalog lives in @opentabs-dev/shared so the side panel can render browser tools instantly on mount without waiting for a server round-trip, permanently eliminating the jitter/flash problem.

userStories.title: Create build-time catalog generator script
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want a build script that imports the compiled browser tools barrel and generates a static catalog file in the shared package, so that browser tool metadata has a single source of truth with zero manual maintenance.
acceptanceCriteria: A script exists at scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts
The script dynamically imports platform/mcp-server/dist/browser-tools/index.js (the tsc-compiled barrel) to get the actual BrowserToolDefinition objects
The script extracts only { name, description, icon } from each tool — icon defaults to 'globe' when absent
The script sorts entries alphabetically by name
The script writes platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts with a readonly BrowserToolMeta[] export named BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG, plus the BrowserToolMeta interface
The generated file includes a header comment stating it is auto-generated and should not be edited manually
The BrowserToolMeta interface has all three fields required (not optional): name, description, icon (the default is applied at generation time)
The generated file is re-exported from platform/shared/src/index.ts
If the generated output matches the existing file content, the script exits without writing (avoids unnecessary git diffs)
A generate:browser-tools-catalog script is added to root package.json that runs: tsx scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts

The root build script is updated to run generate:browser-tools-catalog after tsc --build but before build:bundle (since the generated file is committed to git, tsc --build succeeds with the existing committed version; the generator then verifies/updates it before esbuild bundles the extension)
Running the script produces a file with exactly 40 entries (one per browser tool)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Replace BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES with BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG validation
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the static BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES list and its validation replaced by BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG, so there is one fewer manually maintained artifact and the catalog is validated against actual definitions at server startup.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tool-names.ts is deleted

platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts validates against BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG (imported from @opentabs-dev/shared) instead of BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES — checks name, description, and icon match for every tool
The validation throws a clear error message if a tool's metadata doesn't match its catalog entry (mentioning which field differs)
The validation throws if a tool exists in the definitions but not the catalog, or vice versa
platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts switches from importing BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES to using BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG.map(t => t.name) for prompt injection detection (the checkBrowserToolReferences function)

e2e/helpers.ts replaces its hand-maintained BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES duplicate with an import from @opentabs-dev/shared: import { BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG } from '@opentabs-dev/shared' and derives the name array: BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG.map(t => t.name)
All E2E test files that import BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES from e2e/helpers.ts still work without changes (the export name stays the same or all import sites are updated)
No remaining imports of browser-tool-names anywhere in the codebase
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Three consumers of BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES need updating:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (lines 49, 95-108) — currently imports BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES and validates name-only sync. Replace with BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG import from @opentabs-dev/shared and validate all three fields (name, description, icon). The icon comparison should handle the default: tool.icon ?? 'globe' should match catalogEntry.icon.

2. platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts (line 12, used at line 104) — imports BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES for checkBrowserToolReferences(). Replace with: import { BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG } from '@opentabs-dev/shared'; and use BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG.map(t => t.name) to build the Set. Consider caching the derived name set in a module-level const to avoid re-mapping on every call.

3. e2e/helpers.ts (lines 219-260) — has a hand-maintained duplicate of all tool names. Replace the entire block with: import { BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG } from '@opentabs-dev/shared'; export const BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES = BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG.map(t => t.name); This preserves the export name so the 8+ E2E test files that import BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES from helpers.ts continue to work unchanged.

Note: e2e/ has its own tsconfig (e2e/tsconfig.json). Verify it can resolve @opentabs-dev/shared — check if it has a project reference to platform/shared or resolves via node_modules. If neither, add the reference.

After deleting browser-tool-names.ts, run knip to confirm no dead code warnings. The file is currently only imported by index.ts and loader.ts, both of which will be updated.

userStories.title: Initialize side panel browserTools state from static catalog
userStories.description: As a user, I want the browser tools card to render instantly when I open the side panel, so there is no flash of empty state or layout shift while waiting for the server response.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx initializes browserTools state from BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG (imported from @opentabs-dev/shared) with all tools defaulting to enabled: true
The BrowserToolsCard renders on the first frame when connected — no waiting for config.getState response
When config.getState returns, only the enabled field is merged from the server response into the existing state (name, description, icon remain from the static catalog)
The merge handles the case where the server response has fewer or more tools than the catalog (defensive — should never happen in practice but handle gracefully)
Search/filter for browser tools works immediately without waiting for server data (since name, description, icon are already populated)
Tool toggle (enable/disable) still works correctly — optimistic update + server round-trip + reconciliation
activeTools tracking (tool.invocationStart/End) still works correctly
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Remove NoPluginsState and simplify loading gates
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the NoPluginsState component and its associated loading gates removed, since browser tools are always present and the empty state can never occur when connected.
acceptanceCriteria: NoPluginsState is removed from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.tsx
The pluginsLoaded state variable is removed from App.tsx (it was added specifically to gate NoPluginsState)
The showNoPlugins conditional is removed from App.tsx
The render branch for NoPluginsState in App.tsx is removed
hasContent in App.tsx is simplified — it is always true when browserTools.length > 0 (which is always true from static catalog), but the check can remain as a defensive guard
The LoadingState spinner is still shown while waiting for the initial connection (before connected becomes true)
DisconnectedState still renders correctly when the server is unreachable or auth fails
The Storybook story for NoPluginsState is removed (if it exists in EmptyStates.stories.tsx)

E2E tests that assert 'No Plugins Installed' is hidden are updated — the assertions can be removed since the text no longer exists in the DOM at all, or they can remain as-is (asserting hidden/not-visible still passes if the element doesn't exist)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
This story removes dead code that becomes unreachable after US-003.

In App.tsx:
- Line 57: Remove `const [pluginsLoaded, setPluginsLoaded] = useState(false);`
- Line 345: Remove `const showNoPlugins = pluginsLoaded && !hasContent && !searchQuery;`
- Lines 384-385: Remove the `showNoPlugins ? <NoPluginsState />` render branch
- Find all places where `setPluginsLoaded(true)` is called (in the fetchConfigState success handler) and remove them
- The `hasContent` variable (line 342) can stay as-is: `plugins.length > 0 || failedPlugins.length > 0 || browserTools.length > 0` — it's always true because browserTools is always populated, but it's a clean defensive check

In EmptyStates.tsx:
- Remove the NoPluginsState component (lines 70-79)
- Remove it from the export (line 83)
- Keep DisconnectedState and LoadingState

In EmptyStates.stories.tsx:
- Remove the NoPluginsState story if one exists

E2E tests that reference 'No Plugins Installed':
- e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts line 153: `await expect(sidePanelPage.locator('text=No Plugins Installed')).toBeHidden()` — this assertion still passes because .toBeHidden() returns true when the element doesn't exist in the DOM. Can be left as-is or removed for clarity.
- e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts line 134: `await expect(sidePanelPage.locator('text=No Plugins Installed')).toBeHidden()` — same situation.
- e2e/side-panel-auth-failed.e2e.ts line 52: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('No Plugins Installed')).not.toBeVisible()` — .not.toBeVisible() also passes when element doesn't exist.

All three negative assertions still pass without changes, but consider removing them since they test a condition that can no longer occur. This makes the tests more honest — they don't assert about UI that no longer exists.

Also check: the onboarding.e2e.ts test named 'removing all plugins shows no-plugins card' (line 164). This test name references a concept that no longer exists. The test body asserts `text=Browser` is visible (which still passes since browser tools are always there). Consider renaming the test to reflect the new behavior: 'removing all plugins still shows browser tools card'.

This is the E2E checkpoint story because it changes browser-observable behavior (side panel rendering) and is the final story that touches UI.
